name: Shawnee Tva Employees
description_short: TVA's Shawnee Fossil Plant is a coal-fired power generation facility near Paducah, Kentucky, employing 224 workers to produce electricity for the regional grid since 1953.
description_long: Shawnee Fossil Plant is operated by the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA), a federal utility agency, and has been generating electricity since 1953 at a location 10 miles northwest of Paducah, Kentucky, at the confluence of the Ohio and Cumberland rivers. The plant was constructed to meet national defense industry power demands, particularly for the nearby Atomic Energy Commission's Paducah uranium enrichment facility during the Cold War era. Originally designed as a 10-unit facility, Shawnee was the second-largest coal-fired plant in the U.S. upon completion in 1956. Shawnee Fossil Plant provides bulk electricity generation to regional customers across TVA's service territory. The facility operates nine active generating units with a combined summer net capacity of 1,206 megawatts, capable of powering more than 640,000 homes. The plant generates approximately 8 billion kilowatt-hours of electricity annually. TVA has invested in environmental controls, including flue gas desulfurization systems on Units 1 and 4 completed in 2017, and is pursuing renewable energy projects such as the 100-megawatt Project Phoenix solar pilot on a closed coal ash site. Shawnee's operational track record includes Unit 6's national record for continuous coal-fired plant operation at 1,093 days, 11 hours, and 24 minutes. The facility is listed on the National Register of Historic Places (2016) and has been recognized for its role in TVA's post-World War II fossil steam plant development. The plant's 224-person workforce is engaged in both power generation and community service initiatives including educational tours, charitable drives, and local nonprofit support. This is a government-operated power generation facility, not a consumer finance company. The categorization as "payday-alternatives" was incorrect—Shawnee Fossil Plant is an electricity utility providing bulk power to regional customers, more appropriately categorized as a banking/utility infrastructure entity. This profile serves informational purposes only and has no relevance to consumer financial services.
services: Bulk electricity generation via nine active coal-fired generating units
Summer net generating capacity of 1,206 megawatts for regional distribution
Baseload power production generating approximately 8 billion kilowatt-hours annually
Flue gas desulfurization environmental control systems reducing sulfur emissions
Water quality and air quality monitoring and environmental compliance
Educational facility tours for students and community members
Community charitable programs including food drives and toy drives
Project Phoenix solar generation project development on reclaimed coal ash sites
Coal ash site closure and environmental remediation
Infrastructure and grid stability services to regional customers
Historical preservation as National Register of Historic Places facility
pros: Demonstrates exceptional operational reliability with Unit 6 holding the national continuous operation record for coal-fired plants at 1,093+ days without stopping
Generates 1,206 megawatts of summer capacity, providing affordable baseload electricity to 640,000+ homes across the region
Invested in environmental protections including flue gas desulfurization systems removing up to 96% of sulfur emissions
Pursuing renewable energy innovation through Project Phoenix, a 100-megawatt solar pilot project on reclaimed coal ash sites
Established community engagement programs including student educational tours, food/supply drives, and support for local nonprofits
Recognized as a National Register of Historic Places facility for its significance in post-WWII American infrastructure development
Strategically located at the confluence of the Ohio and Cumberland rivers on 1,696 acres, enabling efficient water access and transportation
cons: Coal-fired generation contributes to carbon emissions and air quality concerns despite environmental control investments
Dependent on coal supply chain economics and subject to increasing regulatory pressure on fossil fuel plants
Historical reliance on a single major customer (Paducah uranium enrichment plant) until its 2013 closure reduced facility's original strategic purpose
Retirement of Unit 10 in 2014 reduced overall facility capacity and represents ongoing industry shift away from coal
Environmental legacy includes coal ash management challenges requiring ongoing remediation and closure protocols
best_for: Not applicable—this is a power utility, not a consumer finance service
Regional electricity consumers in TVA service territory seeking reliable baseload power generation
Researchers studying post-WWII American industrial infrastructure and energy policy
Students and community members interested in power generation education and utility operations
diagnosis: This is a government-operated coal-fired power plant operated by the Tennessee Valley Authority, not a consumer finance company. The original "payday-alternatives" categorization is entirely incorrect. Shawnee Fossil Plant provides bulk electricity generation infrastructure and has no relationship to consumer lending, credit products, or financial services of any kind.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
